title: Getting started with shooters
text: ns_tree() performs four steps every .R file finds target directory: Parse — parse() turns file expression list. Extract — extract_func_def() identifies top-level function assignments (name <- function(...) { ... }). Walk — find_calls() recursively walks function body records references known (.e. locally defined) functions. Render — build_tree() traverses resulting call graph depth-first root node, render_tree() formats nested list box-drawing ASCII art. root node resolved three-tier fallback:

title: Getting started with shooters
text: shooters bundles four example apps inst/apps/ cover common Shiny structural patterns. path app directory can retrieved system.file().

what: 1. minimal — bare ui / server, no functions
title: Getting started with shooters
text: simplest possible Shiny app: ui plain R object (function), server function defined top level, passed directly shinyApp(). launcher wrapper. launch() function, ns_tree() falls Tier 2: looks ui_fun server_fun name. ui function definition extract_func_def() skips ; server captured. Pass actual names used file via ui_fun / server_fun arguments:
code: # inst/apps/minimal/app.R ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Minimal App"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(sliderInput("n", "Number of points", min = 10, max = 200, value = 50)),     mainPanel(plotOutput("plot"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$plot <- renderPlot({     d <- data.frame(x = rnorm(input$n), y = rnorm(input$n))     plot(d$x, d$y, pch = 19, col = "steelblue")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) ns_tree(   file.path(apps, "minimal"),   ui_fun     = "ui",   server_fun = "server" ) #> █─(app) #> └─█─server

what: 2. no_modules — helper functions, no Shiny modules
title: Getting started with shooters
text: app split launch() entry point, app_ui(), app_server(), two plain helper functions (make_data(), render_scatter()). NS() moduleServer() used. launch() found immediately (Tier 1), tree rooted . helper calls inside app_server appear children:
code: # inst/apps/no_modules/app.R  (selected functions) app_server <- function(input, output, session) {   plot_data <- make_data(input)   output$scatter <- render_scatter(plot_data) }  make_data <- function(input) { ... } render_scatter <- function(data) { ... }  launch <- function() {   shinyApp(ui = app_ui(), server = app_server) } ns_tree(file.path(apps, "no_modules")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   ├─█─make_data #>   └─█─render_scatter

title: Getting started with shooters
text: scatter-plot module (scatter_ui / scatter_server) defined file called app-level app_ui() / app_server(). tree shows module pair nested one level app_ui / app_server:
code: # inst/apps/single_module/app.R  (selected functions) scatter_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   tagList(sliderInput(ns("n"), ...), plotOutput(ns("plot"))) }  scatter_server <- function(id) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) { ... }) }  app_ui <- function() {   fluidPage(titlePanel("Single Module App"), scatter_ui("scatter1")) }  app_server <- function(input, output, session) {   scatter_server("scatter1") }  launch <- function() shinyApp(ui = app_ui(), server = app_server) ns_tree(file.path(apps, "single_module")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> │ └─█─scatter_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   └─█─scatter_server

title: Getting started with shooters
text: realistic pattern: display parent module owns slider delegates rendering two child modules (plot, table). module pair lives file; app.R sources . ns_tree() reads .R files directory full cross-file call graph assembled tree built: tree faithfully mirrors physical module hierarchy: display parent bridges app level plot table leaf modules.
code: inst/apps/nested_modules/ ├── app.R          # launch(), sources everything ├── app_ui.R       # app_ui()  → display_ui() ├── app_server.R   # app_server() → display_server() ├── mod_display.R  # display_ui(), display_server() → plot_*, table_* ├── mod_plot.R     # plot_ui(), plot_server() └── mod_table.R    # table_ui(), table_server() ns_tree(file.path(apps, "nested_modules")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> │ └─█─display_ui #> │   ├─█─plot_ui #> │   └─█─table_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   └─█─display_server #>     ├─█─plot_server #>     └─█─table_server

title: Getting started with shooters
text: Adjust app_fun, ui_fun, server_fun match whatever naming convention target app uses.
code: ns_tree(   path       = "R",          # directory to scan (default: "R/")   app_fun    = "launch",     # Tier-1 root function name   ui_fun     = "app_ui",     # Tier-2 UI co-root name   server_fun = "app_server"  # Tier-2 server co-root name )

title: Shiny Utility Functions
text: ns_tree() operates four steps: Parse every .R file target directory parse(). Extract top-level function definitions (name <- function(...)). Walk function body find references locally defined functions, building call graph. Render call graph depth-first ASCII tree, starting app root. root resolved three-tier fallback:

title: Shiny Utility Functions
text: Run bundled example apps interactively:

what: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: build_tree() performs depth-first traversal call graph starting given node, producing nested list structure suitable rendering.

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: node Character scalar; root function name. call_graph Named list element character vector function names called function. visited Character vector already-visited node names (used internally prevent cycles).

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph — build_tree
text: nested list elements name (character) children (list similar structures).

what: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: extract_func_def() checks whether parsed expression top-level function assignment (e.g., name <- function(...) { ... }) returns function name body.

title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: expr parsed R expression.

title: Extract function definitions from expression — extract_func_def
text: list elements name (character) body (expression), NULL expression function definition.

what: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: find_calls() recursively walks parsed R expression collects names functions appear either direct calls (fun()) bare name references (fun).

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: expr parsed R expression. known_names Character vector function names look .

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression — find_calls
text: Character vector unique matched function names.

what: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
title: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
text: ns_tree() statically parses R source files directory, builds call graph every function defined , prints plain-text tree showing functions call one another. Functions use shiny::NS() shiny::moduleServer() identified Shiny modules, presence required — ns_tree() works collection R source files.

title: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
text: path Character scalar; path directory containing R source files. Defaults "R". app_fun Character scalar; name top-level app launch function used tree root. Defaults "launch". ui_fun Character scalar; name UI function. Defaults "app_ui". server_fun Character scalar; name server function. Defaults "app_server".

title: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
text: Invisibly returns nested tree structure (list). tree printed console side effect.

title: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
text: tree root resolved three-tier fallback: app_fun — named launcher function (e.g. launch()). ui_fun + server_fun — displayed co-roots (app) launcher found (e.g. bare ui / server objects). defined functions listed flat (app) neither found.

title: Display a plain-text namespace tree for a Shiny app — ns_tree
code: # Locate the bundled demo apps apps <- system.file("apps", package = "shooters")  # Bare ui/server — no wrapper functions, no modules ns_tree(file.path(apps, "minimal"), ui_fun = "ui", server_fun = "server") #> █─(app) #> └─█─server   # Helper functions, no Shiny modules ns_tree(file.path(apps, "no_modules")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   ├─█─make_data #>   └─█─render_scatter   # One NS / moduleServer pair ns_tree(file.path(apps, "single_module")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> │ └─█─scatter_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   └─█─scatter_server   # Modules calling other modules (multi-file) ns_tree(file.path(apps, "nested_modules")) #> █─launch #> ├─█─app_ui #> │ └─█─display_ui #> │   ├─█─plot_ui #> │   └─█─table_ui #> └─█─app_server #>   └─█─display_server #>     ├─█─plot_server #>     └─█─table_server

what: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: render_tree() takes nested tree structure (produced build_tree()) returns character vector lines using box-drawing characters style lobstr::ast().

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
code: render_tree(tree, prefix = "", is_last = TRUE, is_root = TRUE)

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: tree nested list name children elements. prefix Character scalar; current indentation prefix (used internally recursion). is_last Logical; whether node last sibling. is_root Logical; whether node root.

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art — render_tree
text: Character vector formatted lines.

what: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
title: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
text: Launches one example Shiny applications bundled shooters inst/apps/. app demonstrates different structural pattern use ns_tree():

title: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
text: Character scalar; demo app launch. One "minimal", "no_modules", "single_module", "nested_modules" (default).

title: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
text: Called side effect (launches Shiny app). Returns invisibly.

title: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
text: "minimal" — bare ui / server objects passed directly shinyApp(), wrapper functions modules. "no_modules" — helper functions wrapping ui server, Shiny modules. "single_module" — one module pair (NS + moduleServer). "nested_modules" — modules call modules, spread across multiple files.

title: Launch a demo Shiny app — run_demo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ run_demo()                   # nested_modules (default) run_demo("single_module") run_demo("no_modules") run_demo("minimal") } # }

what: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
text: uses_ns() recursively walks parsed R expression detect calls shiny::NS() shiny::moduleServer(), reliable indicators function Shiny module.

title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
text: expr parsed R expression.

title: Check if expression contains Shiny module identifiers — uses_ns
text: Logical scalar; TRUE call NS() moduleServer() found.
